content:
@Gary Guo mentioned about some difficulties of sending patches with SMTP.

Actually there is already a tool allows sending patches without SMTP/MTA (still in experiment). For those who are interested, the tool is

https://pypi.org/project/b4/

and I believe the following two options of b4 send are for web patch submission

--web-auth-new
--web-auth-verify VERIFY_TOKEN

For more information, the author describe the workflow at: https://lore.kernel.org/tools/20220902191622.rrcbq2obitnumfdb@nitro.local/
After reading the instruction mail, tt feels like more work than configuring SMTP, but to each their own. :-)
I think it may be useful for those without access to SMTP (e.g. ProtonMail in the free tier).
Yes definitely

Tried it today, not working somehow:

Converted the branch to 1 messages
Populating To/Cc addresses
Will send the following messages:
---
To: Miguel Ojeda <ojeda@kernel.org>
    Alex Gaynor <alex.gaynor@gmail.com>
    Wedson Almeida Filho <wedsonaf@gmail.com>
    Boqun Feng <boqun.feng@gmail.com>
    Björn Roy Baron <bjorn3_gh@protonmail.com>
Cc: Gary Guo <gary@garyguo.net>
---
  [PATCH] rust: make compiler-builtin stubs non-global
    +Cc: rust-for-linux@vger.kernel.org
         linux-kernel@vger.kernel.org
---
Press Enter to send or Ctrl-C to abort
---
Sending via web endpoint https://lkml.kernel.org/_b4_submit
Error from endpoint: This service only accepts patches
---
CRITICAL: Was not able to send messages.
Guess I'll still have to setup a SMTP proxy of some sort that supports OAuth2 properly.
Are you sending a patch? If so, how is it generated?
Yeah, I am sending a patch. It's just generated by b4 prep -e rust-next, b4 prep --auto-to-cc and b4 prep --edit-cover.
I looked at what b4 generates following its steps. The info looks sensible.
It is just complaining something about signing when I try to send emails.
I configured a smtp proxy and now I can use b4 to send patches. It might just be the web submission interface having some weird filter to prevent abuse.
You patches have not shown up on the mailing list btw.
:upside_down:
Apparently it reached Alex's inbox because Alex replied. The reply is on rust-for-linux@.
I checked the proxy log and it doesn't seem to include the mailing list as recipient.. very weird, not sure if b4 or the proxy is at fault.
Did it drop all the CC lines?
It includes myself, but not the mailing lists.
Looks like a b4 bug.
Kinda expected? The sending feature is experimental after all.
I guess since I already have the SMTP proxy working I could just revert to the good old workflow.
Sending with git send-email works fine. So it's a b4 bug.

Gary Guo said:

Sending with git send-email works fine. So it's a b4 bug.

Konstantin is actively developing b4, so you may want to report it to tools@linux.kernel.org
(I think @bjorn3 wanted to try the web submission endpoint in the future, so it would help him too.)
